explanation: This property ensures that if the self-test fails, then in the next state, the system provides an out-of-service warning and simultaneously prevents certain normal operational modes and actions from being active. The 'out-of-service' state, combined with the negation of key operational modes (PSV mode, PCV mode, Standby mode, active ventilation, Recruitment Maneuver, inspiratory pause, or expiratory pause), captures the requirement that 'any other operations shall be not allowed'.
bool_exp3: OutOfServiceWarning & !(PSVMode | PCVMode | StandbyMode | ventilating | RM | inspiratoryPause | expiratoryPause)

explanation: The property states that globally, if the self-test fails (bool_exp1), then it must always be true that the system is out-of-service (bool_exp3). This captures the immediate warning and the implied non-operational state. The phrase "In addition, any other operations shall be not allowed" is interpreted as a descriptive consequence of being "out-of-service," which is encapsulated by the `OutOfServiceWarning` atomic proposition, implying that normal operations cease.

The property states that globally, if the self-test fails, then the system must issue an 'out-of-service' warning AND transition to a 'fail-safe mode'. The 'fail-safe mode' inherently implies that other operations are not allowed, as described by its atomic proposition definition (in valve closed and out valve open). This captures both parts of the requirement: the warning and the cessation of other operations by entering a restricted mode.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the self-test fails (bool_exp1 becomes true), then in the very next state (X), the system shall indicate an 'out-of-service' warning (bool_exp3 becomes true). The phrase 'any other operations shall be not allowed' is interpreted as an inherent characteristic of the 'out-of-service' state, meaning that when the 'OutOfServiceWarning' is active, normal operations are implicitly prohibited.

The property specifies that globally (G), if the self-test fails (bool_exp1: selfTestFail), then simultaneously the system must issue an out-of-service warning (OutOfServiceWarning) and enter a fail-safe mode (failSafeMode). Entering the fail-safe mode covers the requirement that 'any other operations shall be not allowed', as described in its atomic proposition definition (in valve closed and out valve open).

The property states that globally (G), if the 'selfTestFail' condition (self-test fails) occurs, it must always imply (->) that an 'OutOfServiceWarning' is issued. This directly translates the conditional requirement: 'If the self-test fails, the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service'. The additional clause 'any other operations shall be not allowed' is implicitly covered by the system being in an 'out-of-service' state, as this state naturally implies cessation of normal operations.

The property states that globally, if the self-test fails, then it will always be the case from that point onwards that the system displays an out-of-service warning and no ventilation operations are allowed. This captures both the consequence of the warning and the persistent state of no operations being allowed.

The property states that globally, whenever the self-test fails, the system must immediately (in the same state) issue an out-of-service warning and enter a fail-safe mode. The 'fail-safe mode' inherently implies that 'any other operations shall be not allowed'. This captures the safety requirement that a self-test failure leads to a specific, restricted system state.

The property states that globally, if the self-test fails, then it must be the case that a warning for the system being out-of-service is displayed AND the system enters a fail-safe mode. The fail-safe mode inherently implies that other operations are not allowed, capturing the 'any other operations shall be not allowed' part of the requirement.

The property states that globally, if the self-test fails (selfTestFail), then a conjunctive state must be maintained: either for a fixed duration (N_DURATION) or forever. This conjunctive state requires that the 'Out Of Service' warning is displayed (OutOfServiceWarning) AND that a set of critical operations (ventilating, changing settings, patient management, etc.) are NOT allowed. The use of 'G[0,N_DURATION] P | G P' ensures that once the self-test fails, the system transitions to and remains in this warned and restricted state for at least a certain period, or indefinitely, reflecting a severe system failure.
bool_exp3: OutOfServiceWarning & !(ventilating | ventilatorSettingsChanged | alarmSettingsChanged | PSVModeSelected | RMButton | expiratoryPauseButton | inspiratoryPauseButton | newPatient | patientChanged | resumeVentilation | confirmPSVParameters | enterAlarmThresholds | enableLeakCompensation)
